name: Slice 1: Hook script — state files + terminal tab titles
decisions_by_ai: Added version: 1 field to state files as recommended in plan open question #3 for future-proofing
Used printf with \033 for OSC 0 escape sequences (portable across shells)
Sanitized session_id for filename safety by replacing non-alphanumeric chars (except dash/underscore) with underscores — prevents path traversal attacks
Server auto-launch logic (A1.7, A1.8) deferred to Slice 3 per plan — not included in this slice
diversions_from_plan: Markdown stripping uses sed range deletion for code fences (```...```) instead of single-line pattern, since multiline code blocks span multiple lines
The strip_markdown function uses printf instead of echo to handle escape sequences correctly from jq output
aha_moments: sed uses \( \) for capture groups in basic mode, not ( ) — the initial implementation had invalid backreferences for link/image patterns
jq -r properly converts JSON \n to real newlines, but sed line-anchored patterns (^#) need the input to have actual newlines to work — printf '%s' preserves them correctly
test_instructions: 4. Verify status changed to 'working' in the JSON file
5. Run: echo '{"session_id":"test-123","last_assistant_message":"## Done\n**All** tests pass"}' | bash hooks/tab-state.sh stop
6. Verify status is 'done' and last_message_preview has markdown stripped
8. Verify the state file is deleted
9. To test with real Claude Code, add hook config to ~/.claude/settings.json per the PRD hook configuration section
